speaker: Brian Holman (guest lecturer)
guest_lecture_note: This lecture was delivered by guest lecturer Brian Holman, not by Tom Eagar. Topic: critical airplane engine components and the United Airlines Flight 232 (Sioux City, 1989) case study. Tom is the course instructor; the speaker references prior content from 'Professor Eagar.'
primary_topics: turbine and compressor section materials (Ni-base superalloys, Ti-6Al-4V)
engine life-limited parts (LLPs) and FAA safe-life methodology
titanium alloy metallurgy (alpha/beta phases, Widmanstätten structure)
manufacturing defects (hard alpha inclusions, segregation, white spots, freckles)
failure analysis (ratchet marks, beach marks, fractography)
United Airlines Flight 232 case study (Sioux City, July 1989)
primary_case: United Airlines Flight 232 / Sioux City, Iowa, 1989 (CF6-6 engine, hard alpha inclusion in titanium fan disc)
flagged_for_editorial_review: Speaker identity: guest lecture, not Eagar
